body.content:
You don't often get email from nmazumder@atlassian.com. Learn why this is important
Hi Adam,

I’m reaching out because Atlassian recently brought several capabilities together into a new Service Collection, which now includes Jira Service Management along with additional products for:
  • Faster employee and customer support
  • Better visibility across requests and services
  • Stronger collaboration between IT and business teams
I’d love to learn more about your current service workflows and see if a quick overview of the Service Collection would be helpful for your team.
Would you be open to a brief 20–30 minute conversation in the next week or two?

Thanks,
Naveed


Naveed Mazumder
Solution Sales Executive, Service Collection
(647) 761-8411

I’d love to better understand how your teams are using JSM today, where you’re seeing gaps or friction, and how we can align the roadmap and best practices to your priorities. To that end, I’d like to schedule a brief introductory call.

Proposed agenda (30 minutes):
  • Quick introductions and your role/priorities
  • Current use of Jira Service Management (what’s working / what’s not)
  • Key initiatives and goals for your ITSM / service delivery roadmap
  • Opportunities to optimize or expand your use of JSM
  • Next steps and recommended follow‑up
Would you be open to a short call next week? You can pick a time that works best for you HERE.

Or, feel free to reply with a few times that work on your end and I’ll send a calendar invite.

From: Sergio Bernardou <sbernardou@centrilogic.com>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 11:51 AM
To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

 

sbernardou@centrilogic.com

www.centrilogic.com

body.content:

Hi Adam,

I wanted to make a quick introduction. I work with companies that need reliable development support without the overhead of building a full internal team.

I represent a development team I’ve worked with for over six years—first as a customer, and now as their U.S. client representative. The engineering team is based in Pakistan, and I remain directly involved here in the U.S. to ensure clear communication, proper oversight, and long-term support.

To make it easy to evaluate us, I’m offering a free 40-hour pilot—one week of dev work, no commitment.
Would an extra 40 hours help on a current project?

Best regards,

John Harris | Business Development Manager
Techliance USA

Your message to Marc.Pare@aptum.com couldn't be delivered.
Marc.Pare wasn't found at aptum.com.
Adam.Nelson Office 365 Marc.Pare
Action Required Recipient
Unknown To address

How to Fix It
The address may be misspelled or may not exist. Try one or more of the following:
  • Send the message again following these steps: In Outlook, open this non-delivery report (NDR) and choose Send Again from the Report ribbon. In Outlook on the web, select this NDR, then select the link "To send this message again, click here." Then delete and retype the entire recipient address. If prompted with an Auto-Complete List suggestion don't select it. After typing the complete address, click Send.
  • Contact the recipient (by phone, for example) to check that the address exists and is correct.
  • The recipient may have set up email forwarding to an incorrect address. Ask them to check that any forwarding they've set up is working correctly.
  • Clear the recipient Auto-Complete List in Outlook or Outlook on the web by following the steps in this article: Fix email delivery issues for error code 5.1.10 in Office 365, and then send the message again. Retype the entire recipient address before selecting Send.
If the problem continues, forward this message to your email admin. If you're an email admin, refer to the More Info for Email Admins section below.

Was this helpful? Send feedback to Microsoft.

More Info for Email Admins
Status code: 550 5.1.10

This error occurs because the sender sent a message to an email address hosted by Office 365 but the address is incorrect or doesn't exist at the destination domain. The error is reported by the recipient domain's email server, but most often it must be fixed by the person who sent the message. If the steps in the How to Fix It section above don't fix the problem, and you're the email admin for the recipient, try one or more of the following:

The email address exists and is correct - Confirm that the recipient address exists, is correct, and is accepting messages.

Synchronize your directories - If you have a hybrid environment and are using directory synchronization make sure the recipient's email address is synced correctly in both Office 365 and in your on-premises directory.

Errant forwarding rule - Check for forwarding rules that aren't behaving as expected. Forwarding can be set up by an admin via mail flow rules or mailbox forwarding address settings, or by the recipient via the Inbox Rules feature.

Recipient has a valid license - Make sure the recipient has an Office 365 license assigned to them. The recipient's email admin can use the Office 365 admin center to assign a license (Users > Active Users > select the recipient > Assigned License > Edit).

Mail flow settings and MX records are not correct - Misconfigured mail flow or MX record settings can cause this error. Check your Office 365 mail flow settings to make sure your domain and any mail flow connectors are set up correctly. Also, work with your domain registrar to make sure the MX records for your domain are configured correctly.

For more information and additional tips to fix this issue, see Fix email delivery issues for error code 5.1.10 in Office 365.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

 

sbernardou@centrilogic.com

www.centrilogic.com

Follow us: Linkedin | X
